page.title: B-cell chronic lymphocytic leukemia — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for B-cell chronic lymphocytic leukemia: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for B-cell chronic lymphocytic leukemia: 110 alterations and 807 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Ultra-processed food consumption and cancer risk.
Chronic tylenol use is associated wtih an almost two-fold increased risk of blood cancer.
Each 10% increment in the consumption of UPF was associated with a 22% higher odds ratio of chronic lymphocytic leukemia.
Incidence rates for chronic lymphocytic leukemia in the US states are significantly correlated with the levels of residential radon.
Integrative analysis of spontaneous CLL regression highlights genetic and microenvironmental interdependency in CLL.
Mistletoe extract is known to induce apoptosis and immunactivation in chronic lymphocytic leukemia, and has been shown to have a non-propogating effect on leukemic cells.
Rice bran arabinoxylan and curcumin therapy may be of benefit to patients with early-stage B-cell lymphoid malignancies.
The effect of dietary intervention with high-oleocanthal and oleacein olive oil in Patients with early-stage chronic lymphocytic leukemia.
Verbena officinalis essential oil and its component citral has apoptoic-inducing activity against chronic lymphocytic leukemia.
Neem leaf extract exhibits anti-leukemic properties in patient primary chronic lymphocytic leukemia cells.
These results suggest deguelin could have potential for the treatment of human CLL.
A case of complete and durable molecular remission of chronic lymphocytic leukemia following treatment with epigallocatechin-3-gallate.
Beetroot-carrot juice can be used as an effective treatment for CLL alone or in combination with chlorambucil.
"Dietary polyphenols in cancer prevention: the example of the flavonoid quercetin in leukemia."
Genistein increases the efficacy of fludarabine in inducing cell death in blood cells from CLL and PLL patients.
Honokiol-chlorambucil co-prodrugs selectively enhance the killing effect through STAT3 binding on lymphocytic leukemia cells.
Vanillic acid protects mortality and toxicity induced by N-ethyl-N-nitrosourea in mice.
Vitamin D and gossypol appear to reduce lymphocyte content in Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia.
Wogonin treatment prevented leukemia development when given early after transplantation.
"Inhibition of Nuclear Factor Kappa B activation in Early-Stage Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia by Omega-3 Fatty Acids."
Allicin, a highly active component from freshly crushed garlic, induces programmed cell death in B-chronic lymphocytic leukemia tumor cells.
An anthocyanin-rich dietary bilberry extract induced a redox-sensitive apoptosis in CLL cells with little effect on healthy peripheral blood mononuclear cells.
Anti-tumor effect of berberine on chronic lymphocytic leukemia cells.
Apoptotic effect of berberine via Bcl-2, ROR1, and mir-21 in patients with B-chronic lymphocytic leukemia.
Ascorbic acid synergistically enhances the therapeutic effect of targeted therapy in chronic lymphocytic leukemia.
Association of leukemia target genes Tet2, Bcl2, and Slc23a2 in vitamin C pathways.
Berberine affects mitochondrial activity and cell growth of leukemic cells from chronic lymphocytic leukemia patients.
Both fisetin and quercetin were able to augment the cytotoxic activity of luteolin against chronic lymphocytic leukemia cells.
Compounds from chaga mushroom exhibited strong cytotoxicity against A549 tumor cell lines.
Curcumin (from Turmeric) increases the efficacy of chemotherapeutic agents in the treatment of B-CLL.
Curcumin induces apoptosis in primary resting B chronic lymphocytic leukemia cells.
Curcumin induces programmed cell death in chronic lymphocytic leukemia B cells and works synergistically with the green tea polypenol EGCG.
Deguelin induces programmed cell death in B-cell chronic lymphocytic leukemia (B-CLL) cells.
Effects of berberine on leukemia with a focus on its molecular targets.
Gamma-linolenic acid induces apoptosis in B-chronic lymphocytic leukaemia cells in vitro.
Green tea contains phytochemicals (e.g. EGCG) which exhibit significant therapeutic activity in Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia.
Hepatitis viruses may contribute to the pathogenesis of lymphoproliferative disorders.
Honokiol is a potent inducer of apoptosis in B-CLL cells.
Hyperforin (a fat-soluble compound from St. John's wort) may improve B-cell Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia.
Hyperforin induces apoptosis of chronic lymphocytic leukemia cells.

natural_products.key_findings: Human: Case Report: Beetroot-carrot juice can be used as an effective treatment for CLL alone or in combination with chlorambucil.

natural_products.name: 5,7-dihydroxy-3-(4-hydroxyphenyl)chromone
natural_products.key_findings: Animal Study: Genistein increases the efficacy of fludarabine in inducing cell death in blood cells from CLL and PLL patients.

natural_products.name: Vanillic Acid
natural_products.key_findings: Animal Study: Vanillic acid protects mortality and toxicity induced by N-ethyl-N-nitrosourea in mice.

natural_products.name: cottonseed oil (gossypol)
natural_products.key_findings: Animal Study: Vitamin D and gossypol appear to reduce lymphocyte content in Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia.

natural_products.name: Wogonin
natural_products.key_findings: Animal Study, In Vitro Study: Wogonin treatment prevented leukemia development when given early after transplantation.

natural_products.name: Bilberry
natural_products.key_findings: In Vitro Study: An anthocyanin-rich dietary bilberry extract induced a redox-sensitive apoptosis in CLL cells with little effect on healthy peripheral blood mon

natural_products.name: 2-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-3,7-dihydroxy-chromone
natural_products.key_findings: In Vitro Study: Both fisetin and quercetin were able to augment the cytotoxic activity of luteolin against chronic lymphocytic leukemia cells.

natural_products.name: 2-(3,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-5,7-dihydroxy-chromone
natural_products.key_findings: In Vitro Study: Both fisetin and quercetin were able to augment the cytotoxic activity of luteolin against chronic lymphocytic leukemia cells.

natural_products.name: Chaga Mushroom (Inonotus obliquus)
natural_products.key_findings: In Vitro Study: Compounds from chaga mushroom exhibited strong cytotoxicity against A549 tumor cell lines.

natural_products.name: Polyphenols
natural_products.key_findings: In Vitro Study: Curcumin induces programmed cell death in chronic lymphocytic leukemia B cells and works synergistically with the green tea polypenol EGCG.

natural_products.name: Gamma-Linoleic Acid (GLA)
natural_products.key_findings: In Vitro Study: Gamma-linolenic acid induces apoptosis in B-chronic lymphocytic leukaemia cells in vitro.

natural_products.name: Hyperforin
natural_products.key_findings: In Vitro Study: Hyperforin induces apoptosis of chronic lymphocytic leukemia cells.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Early-stage (Rai 0-I): watch-and-wait appropriate in 50-70%; spontaneous remission extremely rare; median time to treatment in Rai Stage 0: 10+ years; Richter's transformation (5-10%) extremely poor prognosis
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Venetoclax + obinutuzumab (CLL14): 2-year fixed duration, MRD-undetectable remission in 36%, PFS benefit sustained at 5 years vs chlorambucil; ibrutinib continuous: PFS benefit but not curative; BRUIN CLL trial (pirtobrutinib): 73% ORR in ibrutinib-resistant CLL
remission.gap_size: Large — chlorambucil still used in some elderly patients; BTK inhibitor choice and venetoclax combination timing are practice-variable
remission.barrier_detail: Chemoimmunotherapy (FCR, BR) largely replaced by BTK inhibitors and venetoclax but chlorambucil still used where cost is a barrier; the 'best drug at first relapse' question is evolving rapidly; CAR-T not yet standard for CLL
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — pirtobrutinib (non-covalent BTKi) for ibrutinib-resistant CLL; 2020 venetoclax + obinutuzumab fixed-duration; 2019 ibrutinib first-line approved; fludarabine + cyclophosphamide + rituximab (FCR) as standard 2006-2019; chlorambucil since 1950s
remission.outdated_treatments: Chlorambucil as sole agent in eligible younger patients (FCR or BTKi clearly superior); CHOP chemotherapy for CLL (not appropriate); rituximab monotherapy (combination with chemotherapy or BTKi superior)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Infection prophylaxis and vaccination (particularly COVID-19 booster strategies for immunocompromised CLL patients); exercise during watch-and-wait; IVIG for hypogammaglobulinaemia prevention of infections
remission.frontier_signal: Pirtobrutinib (BRUIN — non-covalent BTKi overcoming C481S resistance); zanubrutinib + venetoclax combinations; CAR-T for CLL (TRANSCEND CLL 004 — lisocabtagene maraleucel); bispecific antibodies; MRD-guided treatment duration protocols
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
